item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonderfully creepy. Something about Lancelot’s dialogue rubbed me the wrong way as I was reading, but once his true nature was revealed it made perfect sense. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well this is bloody horrifying.

I love it.

Had a bit of a hard time imagining flatworms walking through the woods, but that's just me, otherwise a very interesting article with logs that definitely drew me in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have such a tough time imagining shit but this was so good and I loved it, the dialogue was great too, I had a lot fun reading it.

Also If I ever met someone named Lancelot who acted like that I would just. Run

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this.

It's very easy, in my opinion, to do a "uh oh, Foundation Evil!" story. It's much harder to do one where the Foundation is trying its best but, in a very realistic way, overlooks something. The real horror here, at least to me, is not the spooky monster (though it's certainly an unpleasant anomaly to say the least), it's the fact that the SCP Foundation, rigorous and logical as they are, managed to just give this *thing* playing dress-up as an agent so much control over containment of what turned out to be, basically, itself.

And assuming this is the "current" iteration of the log, in the in-universe year of 2019, it's been almost 30 years and they've yet to capture the thing. Which is a solid metaphor (intentional or not) for how fuckups like the one the Foundation commits here can often have an impact that well outlasts the actual incident.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was me, yeah. I figured if they had integrated medical systems, they'd have had some measure of "okay so this is the third baby with three arms from department b we should deal with that".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exploration log done well with a very scary anomaly. Also, is the Delivery Log your own creation? I like the idea that there's just a "oops, anomalous baby" option on the log +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit. I was definitely not expecting the twist. The buildup and the tension in the video logs was great, and I was legitimately shocked by the ending. This is definitely one of the best reads I have had in a while. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The reveal was one heck of a slow-burn, what with how oddly Lancelot had been behaving in the vid-logs (which I had initially explained away as being a long-time resident of Nx-56, and having had to deal with the anomaly for a while).

The part that didn't hit me until shortly after reading this is that either Lancelot was trained and inducted into the Foundation by the Nexus Site (which, after 4886-AO, you can bet the Foundation protocol changed real quick, or that whatever he is isn't something the Foundation was able to detect until he did something no one could have swept under the rug.

Definite +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm with ARD here, the twist in Lancelot was performed excellently and the dialogue feels genuine, especially knowing what we do about Lancelot now. Looking back on his dialogue, he comes off exactly like a serial killer. Self-important yet charismatic. I'm not sure if I'm misinterpreting that last log but it appears to just unveil the monster beneath Lancelot's skin without much more. Feels like a step back from the calculating Lancelot leading potential victims deeper into his own lair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was damn good. Despite its length I was captivated until the end. Strong +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yowie zowie. I'm placeholderizing this message so, y'know, I remember to read this Goliath my next free evening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was actually thinking of the Flukeman from the episode “The Host” while reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God this would be a hell of a monster of the week.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not a fan of the last log, but on the whole this is a strongly creepy article wonderfully reminiscent of the X-Files.

item.title: author pauthor
item.content:encoded:
I was in the bathroom, I didn't hear him leave
I locked the door behind him and I turned around to see
All the cookie wrappers and the empty cups of tea
Well I sighed and mumbled to myself "Again I have to clean"

Kill your idols, before they kill you.

Thanks to DrLycus for helping me figure out the weight of baby skin (even if I didn't end up using that factoid), kinchtheknifeblade for her advice re: serial killers and crit, Uncle Nicolini, Elogee FishTruck, and The Great Hippo for their fantastic wording critique (and, in Hippo's case, the second image), and David Lynch for teaching me songs without key under moonless nights.

summerstone
forestforthefence
dasha
dash3
